report_name: Traffic Violation Scams Leverage QR Codes to Harvest Financial Data

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: A phishing campaign uses QR codes embedded in fake traffic violation notices to direct victims to malicious websites designed to harvest payment card details. This method is also known as 'quishing'.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: The scam impersonates government agencies and transportation authorities to create a sense of urgency and trick victims into providing their personal and financial information on a fake payment portal.

identities.name: FBI
identities.description: The FBI's Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3) was cited as reporting that government impersonation scams accounted for nearly $800 million in losses in 2025.

identities.name: Government Agencies
identities.description: A class of organizations, including transportation authorities, that are being impersonated by scammers in QR code phishing campaigns to lend legitimacy to fake traffic violation notices.

infrastructure.name: QR Code Phishing Website
infrastructure.description: A high-quality replica of a government payment portal, hosted on domains such as city-pay-tolls.com, designed to harvest personal and payment card details from victims who scan a malicious QR code.

indicators.name: Quishing Phishing Domain city-pay-tolls.com
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Quishing Phishing Domain city-pay-tolls.com
id: 7f5d1b3a-8c9e-4f2d-b1a0-9e8d7c6b5a4f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to a domain known to be used in QR code phishing scams impersonating traffic violation notices.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/scams/2026/04/traffic-violation-scams-swap-links-for-qr-codes-to-steal-your-card-details
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-04
tags:
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - None expected.
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith: '.city-pay-tolls.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to the domain city-pay-tolls.com, which was identified as an example of a malicious site used in QR code phishing scams.

indicators.name: Suspicious DNS Query to .top TLD
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious DNS Query to .top TLD
id: 1e9a8b7c-6d5e-4f3c-b2a1-0e9d8c7b6a5f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains with the .top TLD, which is frequently abused for phishing and other malicious activities. This rule is of low confidence and may generate false positives.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/scams/2026/04/traffic-violation-scams-swap-links-for-qr-codes-to-steal-your-card-details
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-04
tags:
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate websites using the .top TLD.
level: low
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith: '.top'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for domains ending in .top, a pattern noted as low-confidence but associated with malicious campaigns.
